mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI reading below 2.5% indicates stable inflation expectations, reducing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL remains below 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust economic activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: Interaction effect between PAYEMS and INDPRO shows Rank IC < 0.05 in 3-year rolling windows, or 5-day returns decline despite concurrent growth.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 800k signals weakening demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through reduced consumer confidence and corporate investment.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where HOUST remains below 800k.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 7.5% indicates slack in the labor market, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through reduced consumer spending and corporate profit margins.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 55%+ of instances where UNRATE exceeds 7.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A 60-day increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 12-month moving average signals strong manufacturing activity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in 50%+ of instances where INDPRO exceeds its 12mo MA.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real sales (RSAFS) and GDP (GDPC1) indicates strong aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through elevated corporate revenue and profit expectations.
failure_condition: Interaction effect between RSAFS and GDPC1 shows Rank IC < 0.05 in 3-year rolling windows, or 20-day returns decline despite concurrent growth.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below 1.0% signals aggressive monetary easing, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through liquidity injections and reduced borrowing costs.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in 55%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%.
